title: Something Beautiful
notes: The biggest takeaway from this album is Cyrus' maturity. This is a completely different sound, persona, and age from her previous work. Her personas blend together in a really pleasing way, starting with a husky Amy Winehouse sound and going through some Abba, Fleetwood Mac, and more. It's refreshing. It feels like art; a reflection of her journey and emotions from a mature perspective.
highlights: Highlights for me are the artistry and variance in the album. It travels many genres and in every one, Cyrus sounds like she belongs. Her singer songwriter roots from previous albums and her fun grooves are just pleasant to listen to. More than that, I think the album is emotionally cohesive, which is a feat considering its breadth of topics.
lowlights: I don't think this album really has a lowlight. The only criticism I have is that there isn't really a standout track. It is an art piece in its entirety, but no single section of the canvas really stands above the rest. As such, I think its a bit hard to give it more than an 8, but nothing here is below a 7.
